scenario.context: Station 6 of the day: compare offers and justify the decision.
scenario.mission: You compare two workstation offers professionally and economically.

constraints: do not look at price only

customerRequest.subject: Offer comparison station NS-ST-06
customerRequest.body:
Please compare two offers for standard workstations.

We need a short, justified recommendation with a focus on suitability and cost-effectiveness.

questions.title: Identify the better offer
questions.prompt: Offer A: 590 EUR, 8 GB RAM, HDD, 1 video output. Offer B: 660 EUR, 16 GB RAM, SSD, 2 video outputs. Which is more viable for the described office use?

options.text: Offer A, because it is cheaper
options.rationale: Price alone is insufficient when suitability is missing.

options.text: Offer B, because it covers the need better technically
options.rationale: Performance and ports fit the workstation profile.

feedback.wrong: Not optimal: the offer must meet more than price.

questions.title: Criteria for offer comparison
questions.prompt: Which criteria should be included in the decision? (Multiple selection)

options.rationale: Operational safety and follow-up costs influence the decision.

feedback.correct: Correct: you compare holistically and practically.
feedback.wrong: Partially unsuitable: prioritize technical and operational criteria.

questions.title: Total cost per offer
questions.prompt: For 6 workstations: Offer A costs 590 EUR per station plus 35 EUR setup per station. Offer B costs 660 EUR per station plus 20 EUR setup per station. What is the total sum for offer B?

feedback.wrong: Please calculate total per station and multiply by 6.

questions.title: Procedure for offer comparison
items: Briefly align the result with the business unit
correctOrder: Briefly align the result with the business unit

feedback.correct: Correct: the decision path is traceable.
feedback.wrong: Not optimal: criteria first, then comparison and justified recommendation.

questions.title: Short recommendation
questions.prompt: Formulate a short recommendation (4-6 sentences) with technical and economic justification.
questions.expectedOutline: Concise decision brief with criteria reference, cost view and clear recommendation.

feedback.correct: Correct: the decision is traceable and usable.
feedback.wrong: Please connect criteria, costs and recommendation more clearly.
